food_name: Freshly Squeezed Watermelon Juice (No Added Sugar)

local_names.romanized_local: Xīguā Zhī (Chinese); Jus Tembikai (Malay); Tharpoosani Saaru (Tamil)

aliases: Freshly Squeezed Watermelon Juice (No Added Sugar)

english: Freshly Squeezed Watermelon Juice (No Added Sugar)

food_subgroup: Fresh fruit juice — watermelon (Citrullus lanatus), blended and strained, no added sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has watermelon fruit (FOODON:03301352) but does not have a specific term for freshly squeezed watermelon juice. Closest parent is fruit juice. No URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0F9H is the closest FoodEx2 code for watermelon juice; used as closest parent match as no exact code for freshly squeezed hawker-style preparation exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for watermelon juice in clinical terminology; null assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker-stall serving of freshly squeezed watermelon juice is approximately 350 ml (350 g), served cold in a plastic cup or glass over ice.

nutrient_source.title: Watermelon, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 167765)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (ARS) — SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy raw watermelon values used as the basis for freshly squeezed watermelon juice with no added sugar. Juice from blended and strained watermelon retains essentially the same macronutrient and micronutrient profile as raw watermelon flesh per 100 g, with negligible fibre loss accounted for in the dietary_fibre_g value. Values cross-referenced against Singapore HPB Foodo database for contextual validation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Watermelon — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: Watermelon GI reported as 72–80 (mean ~76) in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables. GI value of 76 used as the central estimate. GL per 350 ml serving calculated as: (76 × 26.6 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 20.2 (moderate GL). Available carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 7.6 g/100 g × 350 g / 100 = 26.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (1 mg/100 ml); rich in lycopene and citrulline with vasodilatory properties
hypertension.guidance: Freshly squeezed watermelon juice (no added sugar) is suitable for people with hypertension: it contains only 1 mg sodium per 100 ml, and its citrulline content (~250 mg/100 g) is converted to arginine in the body, a nitric oxide precursor that promotes vasodilation and has been shown in clinical studies to reduce arterial stiffness and systolic blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~76) but moderate glycaemic load (~20.2) per 350 ml serving due to high water content (91%)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume watermelon juice with caution: despite its high GI (~76), a 350 ml serving (no added sugar) delivers only ~26.6 g available carbohydrate, yielding a moderate glycaemic load of ~20.2 — comparable to one medium orange — so small portions without added sugar are preferable to sugar-sweetened alternatives, but portion control and blood glucose monitoring remain essential.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Rich in lycopene (4.5 mg/100 g), citrulline, vitamin C, and potassium; negligible saturated fat and cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Freshly squeezed watermelon juice (no added sugar) is cardioprotective: lycopene (4.5 mg/100 g) inhibits LDL oxidation and reduces inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6), citrulline supports endothelial nitric oxide production, potassium (112 mg/100 ml) supports healthy blood pressure, and the absence of saturated fat and cholesterol makes it a heart-friendly beverage choice.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory lycopene and vitamin C; no pro-inflammatory components
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Watermelon juice (no added sugar) is suitable for people with rheumatoid arthritis: lycopene (4.5 mg/100 g) inhibits NF-κB and reduces CRP and IL-6, vitamin C (~28 mg per 350 ml serving) supports collagen synthesis and antioxidant defence, and the beverage contains no pro-inflammatory components.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Freshly squeezed watermelon juice (西瓜汁; 鲜榨西瓜汁) is a quintessential Singapore hawker beverage — bright red, ice-cold, lightly sweet, and intensely refreshing in Singapore's tropical heat. It is prepared by blending fresh seedless or seeded watermelon (Citrullus lanatus) flesh, straining through a fine sieve or cloth to remove pulp and seeds, and serving immediately over ice in a plastic cup or glass. At hawker juice stalls, sugar syrup is routinely added unless the customer explicitly requests 'no sugar' (无糖 / tanpa gula / சர்க்கரை வேண்டாம்). This record covers the no-added-sugar version only. Nutritionally, watermelon juice is a compelling clinical teaching case: it has a HIGH glycaemic index (~76) because its naturally occurring sugars (6.2 g/100 ml, predominantly fructose and glucose) are dissolved in a very low-fibre aqueous matrix and are absorbed rapidly. However, the glycaemic load per standard 350 ml serving is only ~20.2 (moderate), because the 91% water content dilutes the total carbohydrate to ~26.6 g per serving — comparable to one medium orange. Watermelon is one of the richest dietary sources of lycopene (~4.5 mg/100 g), the carotenoid antioxidant responsible for its red colour, which has documented anti-inflammatory, anti-atherogenic, and mild ACE-inhibitory properties. It also contains citrulline (~250 mg/100 g), an amino acid converted to arginine in the body, which is a precursor to nitric oxide — a potent vasodilator — making watermelon juice genuinely beneficial for cardiovascular and blood pressure health. Per 100 ml: 30 kcal, 0.6 g protein, 0.2 g fat, 7.6 g carbohydrate, 6.2 g sugar, 0.2 g dietary fibre, 1 mg sodium, 112 mg potassium, 8.1 mg vitamin C.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy (raw watermelon, FDC ID 167765), an approved primary source. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables. The juice preparation (blend and strain, no added sugar) does not materially alter the per-100 g nutrient profile relative to raw watermelon flesh. Values are internally consistent and cross-referenced against Singapore HPB Foodo contextual data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are based on USDA SR Legacy raw watermelon (FDC ID 167765) adapted for freshly squeezed juice (blend and strain, no added sugar). The straining process removes most insoluble fibre; dietary_fibre_g (0.2 g/100 ml) reflects the residual soluble fibre in the juice fraction. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — watermelon is a plant food containing no vitamin B12 per USDA FoodData Central). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — watermelon contains no vitamin D per USDA FoodData Central). saturated_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; watermelon flesh contains negligible saturated fatty acids). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — naturally occurring trans fats are absent in watermelon). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant food, no cholesterol). 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.05 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.06 g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy fatty acid profile for raw watermelon. 4. water_g (91.4 g/100 g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy. 5. GI of 76 is the central estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database range of 72–80 for watermelon. GL per 350 ml serving = (76 × 26.6) / 100 = 20.2. 6. Lycopene content (~4.5 mg/100 g) and citrulline content (~250 mg/100 g) cited in health_context are from published literature (Rimando & Perkins-Veazie 2005, J Chromatogr A; Collins et al. 2007, J Agric Food Chem) and are not included as per_100g fields as they are not part of the standard nutrient template. 7. FoodOn URI set to null — no exact FoodOn term for watermelon juice exists; closest parent is fruit juice. SNOMED CT code set to null — no clinical terminology code identified without risk of fabrication. 8. This record covers the no-added-sugar version only. The sugar-added version (standard hawker preparation) would have materially higher sugar_g, energy_kcal, and GI/GL values.
